content.content:
20	** stack across reallocations, doing the work only when needed.
21	** It also allows the running of one GC step when the stack is
22	** reallocated.
23	** 'condmovestack' is used in heavy tests to force a stack reallocation
24	** at every check.
25	*/
26	#define luaD_checkstackaux(L,n,pre,pos)  \
27		if (l_unlikely(L->stack_last.p - L->top.p <= (n))) \
28		  { pre; luaD_growstack(L, n, 1); pos; } \
29	        else { condmovestack(L,pre,pos); }
30	
31	/* In general, 'pre'/'pos' are empty (nothing to save) */
32	#define luaD_checkstack(L,n)	luaD_checkstackaux(L,n,(void)0,(void)0)
33	
34	
35	
36	#define savestack(L,pt)		(cast_charp(pt) - cast_charp(L->stack.p))
37	#define restorestack(L,n)	cast(StkId, cast_charp(L->stack.p) + (n))
38	
39	
40	/* macro to check stack size, preserving 'p' */
41	#define checkstackp(L,n,p)  \
42	  luaD_checkstackaux(L, n, \
43	    ptrdiff_t t__ = savestack(L, p),  /* save 'p' */ \
44	    p = restorestack(L, t__))  /* 'pos' part: restore 'p' */
45	
46	
47	/* macro to check stack size and GC, preserving 'p' */
48	#define checkstackGCp(L,n,p)  \
49	  luaD_checkstackaux(L, n, \
50	    ptrdiff_t t__ = savestack(L, p);  /* save 'p' */ \
51	    luaC_checkGC(L),  /* stack grow uses memory */ \
52	    p = restorestack(L, t__))  /* 'pos' part: restore 'p' */
53	
54	
55	/* macro to check stack size and GC */
56	#define checkstackGC(L,fsize)  \
57		luaD_checkstackaux(L, (fsize), luaC_checkGC(L), (void)0)
58	
59

file.content:
** stack across reallocations, doing the work only when needed.
** It also allows the running of one GC step when the stack is
** reallocated.
** 'condmovestack' is used in heavy tests to force a stack reallocation
** at every check.
*/
#define luaD_checkstackaux(L,n,pre,pos)  \
	if (l_unlikely(L->stack_last.p - L->top.p <= (n))) \
	  { pre; luaD_growstack(L, n, 1); pos; } \
        else { condmovestack(L,pre,pos); }

/* In general, 'pre'/'pos' are empty (nothing to save) */
#define luaD_checkstack(L,n)	luaD_checkstackaux(L,n,(void)0,(void)0)



#define savestack(L,pt)		(cast_charp(pt) - cast_charp(L->stack.p))
#define restorestack(L,n)	cast(StkId, cast_charp(L->stack.p) + (n))


/* macro to check stack size, preserving 'p' */
#define checkstackp(L,n,p)  \
  luaD_checkstackaux(L, n, \
    ptrdiff_t t__ = savestack(L, p),  /* save 'p' */ \
    p = restorestack(L, t__))  /* 'pos' part: restore 'p' */


/* macro to check stack size and GC, preserving 'p' */
#define checkstackGCp(L,n,p)  \
  luaD_checkstackaux(L, n, \
    ptrdiff_t t__ = savestack(L, p);  /* save 'p' */ \
    luaC_checkGC(L),  /* stack grow uses memory */ \
    p = restorestack(L, t__))  /* 'pos' part: restore 'p' */


/* macro to check stack size and GC */
#define checkstackGC(L,fsize)  \
	luaD_checkstackaux(L, (fsize), luaC_checkGC(L), (void)0)

content.content:
160	// ─── StackValue ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
161	
162	/// One slot on the Lua value stack.  Wraps a `LuaValue` and an optional
163	/// to-be-closed delta (for the `tbclist` mechanism).
164	///
165	/// C: `StackValue` in `lobject.h`.
166	/// types.tsv: `StackValue → StackValue { val: LuaValue, tbclist.delta: u16 }`
167	#[derive(Clone)]
168	pub struct StackValue {
169	    // C: StackValue.val — the payload TValue
170	    pub val: LuaValue,
171	    // C: StackValue.tbclist.delta — to-be-closed linked-list delta
172	    pub tbc_delta: u16,
173	}
174	
175	impl Default for StackValue {
176	    fn default() -> Self {
177	        StackValue {
178	            val: LuaValue::Nil,
179	            tbc_delta: 0,
180	        }
181	    }
182	}
183	
184	// ─── CallInfo ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
185	
186	/// Saved state for a Lua or C call frame.
187	///
188	/// C: `struct CallInfo` in `lstate.h`.
189	/// types.tsv: CallInfo → CallInfo (several fields renamed / adapted).
190	///
191	/// The C intrusive doubly-linked list (`previous`, `next` as raw pointers) is
192	/// replaced by `Option<CallInfoIdx>` indices into `LuaState::call_info`.
193	#[derive(Clone)]
194	pub struct CallInfo {
195	    // C: StkIdRel func — stack index of the called function value
196	    // types.tsv: CallInfo.func → StackIdx
197	    pub func: StackIdx,
198	
199	    // C: StkIdRel top — stack-top reservation for this call
200	    // types.tsv: CallInfo.top → StackIdx
201	    pub top: StackIdx,
202	
203	    // C: struct CallInfo *previous
204	    // types.tsv: CallInfo.previous → CallInfoIdx (Option at boundary)
205	    pub previous: Option<CallInfoIdx>,
206	
207	    // C: struct CallInfo *next
208	    // types.tsv: CallInfo.next → CallInfoIdx (Option at tail)
209	    pub next: Option<CallInfoIdx>,
210	
211	    // C: union { struct { savedpc, trap, nextraargs } l; struct { k, old_errfunc, ctx } c; } u
212	    pub u: CallInfoFrame,
213	
214	    // C: union { funcidx, nyield, nres, transferinfo } u2
215	    pub u2: CallInfoExtra,
216	
217	    // C: short nresults
218	    // types.tsv: CallInfo.nresults → i16
219	    pub nresults: i16,
220	
221	    // C: unsigned short callstatus
222	    // types.tsv: CallInfo.callstatus → u16 (bit-packed CIST_* flags)
223	    pub callstatus: u16,
224	}
225	
226	/// Payload of `CallInfo.u`.
227	///
228	/// C: `union { struct l { savedpc, trap, nextraargs }; struct c { k, old_errfunc, ctx } } u`
229	#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
230	pub enum CallInfoFrame {
231	    // C: ci->u.l — Lua function call
232	    Lua {
233	        // C: const Instruction *savedpc → u32 offset into Proto.code
234	        // types.tsv: CallInfo.u.l.savedpc → u32
235	        savedpc: u32,
236	        // C: volatile l_signalT trap
237	        // types.tsv: CallInfo.u.l.trap → bool
238	        trap: bool,
239	        // C: int nextraargs
240	        // types.tsv: CallInfo.u.l.nextraargs → i32
241	        nextraargs: i32,
242	    },
243	    // C: ci->u.c — C function call
244	    C {
245	        // C: lua_KFunction k — continuation for yields
246	        // types.tsv: CallInfo.u.c.k → Option<lua_KFunction>
247	        k: Option<LuaKFunction>,
248	        // C: ptrdiff_t old_errfunc
249	        // types.tsv: CallInfo.u.c.old_errfunc → isize
250	        old_errfunc: isize,
251	        // C: lua_KContext ctx
252	        // types.tsv: CallInfo.u.c.ctx → isize
253	        ctx: isize,
254	    },
255	}
256	
257	/// Continuation function for yieldable C calls.  C: `lua_KFunction`.
258	pub type LuaKFunction = fn(&mut LuaState, status: i32, ctx: isize) -> Result<usize, LuaError>;
259

delta.text: Now I have enough context. The Rust `
LuaState::check_stack` is the `luaD_checkstack` form (raises on error), which is

just the `luaD_checkstackaux` macro with empty pre/pos.

Looking at the existing layout: `stack_last: StackIdx` is
the end-of-stack sentinel and `top: StackIdx` is the current top. The C macro tests `L->stack_last.p -
L->top.p <= n`. If insufficient room, call `grow_stack(n, true)`.

content.text:
Build is green.

**Report:**

- **File modified:** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1113`
- **What it does:** Faithful port of C `luaD_checkstack(L, n)` macro from `ldo.h:32`: asserts `n >= 0`, computes free slots as `stack_last - top`, and if `free <= n` delegates to `grow_stack(n, true)` (raise-on-error form). Mirrors the macro's empty pre/pos semantics.
- **Inner todo!()s that may surface next:** `grow_stack` (line 1114) — `check_stack` calls it when the stack is too small. The very first `check_stack` call during init asks for `LUA_MINSTACK` (20) slots on a fresh stack sized `BASIC_STACK_SIZE` (40), so likely the free-slot branch will pass and `grow_stack` won't fire immediately — but eventually it will be the next panic. Other neighbors (`set_top`, `peek_top`, `peek_at`, `next_ci`, etc.) remain `todo!()` as required.
- **Build status:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is **green** (warnings only, no errors).

result:
Build is green.

**Report:**

- **File modified:** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1113`
- **What it does:** Faithful port of C `luaD_checkstack(L, n)` macro from `ldo.h:32`: asserts `n >= 0`, computes free slots as `stack_last - top`, and if `free <= n` delegates to `grow_stack(n, true)` (raise-on-error form). Mirrors the macro's empty pre/pos semantics.
- **Inner todo!()s that may surface next:** `grow_stack` (line 1114) — `check_stack` calls it when the stack is too small. The very first `check_stack` call during init asks for `LUA_MINSTACK` (20) slots on a fresh stack sized `BASIC_STACK_SIZE` (40), so likely the free-slot branch will pass and `grow_stack` won't fire immediately — but eventually it will be the next panic. Other neighbors (`set_top`, `peek_top`, `peek_at`, `next_ci`, etc.) remain `todo!()` as required.
- **Build status:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is **green** (warnings only, no errors).
